UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BRIAN VIGIL, KATHERINE SEARS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF MOTION TO EXPUNGE NOTICE OF LIS PENDENS AND FOR FEES AND COSTS [CCP 1008] IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN BURDEN. THE PURPORTED NEW FACTS DO NOT DIRECTLY ADDRESS COURT'S FINDING THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN PROBABLE VALIDITY OF THEIR CLAIM. EVEN IF THEY DID, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TO SUSTAIN THEIR BURDEN AGAIN. PLAINTIFFS SHALL RECOVER ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,337.50. ORDER SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK J. RUMSEY, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BRIAN VIGIL & KATHERINE SEARS' MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND FOR FEES AND COSTS IS CONTINUED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON OCTOBER 22, 2013, AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL MATTERS RELATED TO WRONGFUL EVICTIONS PER SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.10. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 (302)

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BRIAN VIGIL, KATHERINE SEARS NOTICE OF MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND FOR FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES A REAL PROPERTY CLAIM PURSUANT TO SFRO 37.9(A)(8); INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS AVAILABLE FOR VIOLATION OF THIS PROVISION PER SFRO 37.9(F). DEFENDANTS CONCEDE THAT MS. SEARS IS NO LONGER RESIDING AT THE PREMISES. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT OFFERING OF THE UNIT FOR RENT TO THIRD PARTIES IS A REQUIRED ELEMENT OF CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION FOR VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.9(A)(8). ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs [Ccp 1008]
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:No appearance by the moving party./ Attorney Jason N. Wolford for Plaintiff Kenneth Yoder the opposing party.

The Court Adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BRIAN VIGIL, KATHERINE SEARS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs [Ccp 1008] is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden. The purported new facts do not directly address Court's finding that Plaintiffs have shown probable validity of their claim. Even if they did, Plaintiffs have submitted evidence to sustain their burden again. Plaintiffs shall recover attorney's fees in the amount of $2,337.50. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)

Noticeof Motion And Moiton For An Undertaking [Ccp 405.34]
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:No appearance by the moving party./ Attorney Jason N. Wolford for Plaintiff Kenneth Yoder the opposing party.

The Court Adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BRIAN VIGIL, KATHERINE SEARS Notice of Motion And Motion For An Undertaking [Ccp 405.34] is DENIED. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lisa Costello, Esq. of The Biernat Law Group, (650-689-5160), for Brian Vigil, Defendant and moving party.
Jason Wolford, Esq. of Law Offices of Jason Wolford, (415-729-5529), for Kenneth Yoder, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Connie J. Parchman, CSR #6137, (510-853-5260).

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BRIAN VIGIL, KATHERINE SEARS Notice Of Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs is DENIED. Plaintiff alleges a real property claim pursuant to SFRO 37.9(a)(8); injunctive relief is available for violation of this provision per SFRO 37.9(f). Defendants concede that Ms. Sears is no longer residing at the premises. Defendants fail to cite any authority for the proposition that offering of the unit for rent to third parties is a required element of cause of action for wrongful eviction for violation of SFRO 37.9(a)(8). Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-11-2013 Continued To Oct-22-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs BRIAN VIGIL & KATHERINE SEARS' Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs is continued to be heard in Department 501 on October 22, 2013, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all matters related to wrongful evictions per San Francisco Local Rule 8.10. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
